items.headline: OpenAI closes $122B funding round at $852B valuation; late-2026 IPO expected
items.summary: OpenAI raised $122 billion in committed capital at an $852B post-money valuation — the largest private funding round ever. With $25B in annualized revenue and projected $14B in losses for 2026, a public listing is increasingly likely this year.

items.headline: DeepSeek V4 to run on Huawei chips; Alibaba, ByteDance, Tencent placing bulk orders
items.summary: China's DeepSeek is launching its next-gen V4 model on Huawei's latest chips — a major signal that China's AI ecosystem can sidestep Nvidia export restrictions. China's largest tech firms are placing hundreds-of-thousands-of-unit orders ahead of launch.

items.headline: Microsoft launches 3 in-house AI models: MAI-Transcribe-1, MAI-Voice-1, MAI-Image-2
items.summary: Microsoft released three proprietary AI models exclusively on Azure Foundry, directly competing with OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic. MAI-Transcribe-1 covers 25 languages at 2.5x the speed of Azure Fast; MAI-Voice-1 generates 60s of audio in 1 second with custom voice cloning support.

items.headline: Google launches Veo 3.1 Lite — most cost-effective AI video model on Vertex AI
items.summary: Google released Veo 3.1 Lite on Vertex AI today: its cheapest video generation model, producing 8-second clips from text or image prompts. Veo 3.1 Fast will see further price cuts starting April 7, with improved prompt understanding and cross-scene consistency.

items.headline: US tech layoffs jump 40% in Q1 2026 — AI now cited for 8% of all job cuts
items.summary: Tech layoffs surged 40% in Q1 2026 vs. prior year. AI is now directly cited for 8% of all cuts (up from 5% in full-year 2025). Oracle cut thousands tied to its AI data center push; Amazon eliminated ~16,000 corporate roles. Total Q1 tech-sector cuts: 217,000+.

items.headline: Mustafa Suleyman: Revised OpenAI contract 'unlocked Microsoft's ability to pursue superintelligence'
items.summary: In a Verge interview, Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman detailed how renegotiating the OpenAI partnership freed Microsoft to build independent frontier AI models. The MAI model trio launched today is the first concrete output of this new strategy.

items.headline: Microsoft plans $10B AI investment in Japan with SoftBank; 1 million engineers by 2030
items.summary: Microsoft announced a $10 billion AI infrastructure commitment in Japan (2026–2029) in partnership with SoftBank — covering AI infrastructure, cybersecurity, and a goal to train 1 million engineers and developers by 2030. Sakura Internet shares jumped 20% on the news.

items.headline: US venture funding hits record $267B; OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI dominate AI dealflow
items.summary: PitchBook data shows US VC funding surged to a record $267 billion, with AI mega-rounds accounting for a disproportionate share. OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI collectively dominated deal value — a trend expected to continue as both OpenAI and Anthropic approach IPO.

items.headline: Google AI Pro expands: 5TB storage, Vids, Lyria music gen, Veo in Workspace
items.summary: Google upgraded its $19.99/month AI Pro plan to 5TB storage (up from 2TB) and rolled out major Workspace additions: Vids AI video creation, Lyria music generation, and Veo video tools integrated into everyday productivity apps like Docs and Slides.

items.headline: Cerebras Systems targeting April 2026 Nasdaq IPO at $22–25B valuation
items.summary: AI chip maker Cerebras Systems — known for wafer-scale processors — is targeting a Nasdaq IPO this month at a $22–25 billion valuation, raising approximately $2 billion. Morgan Stanley is leading the underwriting. Timing may shift depending on market conditions.

items.headline: March jobs: +178K payrolls — 3x the 59K estimate; unemployment falls to 4.3%
items.summary: A blowout March jobs report showed 178,000 payrolls added (vs. 59K expected), completely reversing February's 133K decline. Unemployment fell to 4.3%. Wage growth slowed to +3.5% YoY (lowest since May 2021), easing some inflation concerns — but the combination with surging oil prices still complicates the Fed's path.

items.headline: S&P 500 snaps 5-week losing streak: +3.4% on the week; Nasdaq +4.4%, Dow +3.0%
items.summary: All three major indices broke their five-week skids in this holiday-shortened week, driven by optimism that a negotiated end to the Iran war was possible. Markets are closed today (Good Friday). Thursday's final session: S&P +0.11%, Nasdaq +0.18%, Dow −0.13% on lighter volume.

items.headline: Oil prices surge on Iran war fears; Strait of Hormuz closure raising costs globally
items.summary: Oil prices jumped sharply as the Iran war escalated with today's fighter jet shoot-down. Trump's threats to hit Iranian oil infrastructure and the continued Strait of Hormuz closure are driving energy costs higher for businesses and consumers worldwide.

items.headline: US markets closed for Good Friday; S&P futures down 0.2% as Iran escalates
items.summary: NYSE and Nasdaq are both closed today for the holiday. S&P 500 E-mini futures were down 0.2% this morning as traders assessed Iran war escalation risks and absorbed the stronger-than-expected jobs report released before the market open.

items.headline: Trump budget: $1.5T defense (+42%), begins TSA privatization, slashes social spending
items.summary: The White House's FY budget blueprint calls for $1.5 trillion in defense — a 42% increase — to fund munitions, naval expansion, and the 'Golden Dome' missile shield. Cuts target housing, healthcare, and social services. Small airports must begin using private TSA screeners.

items.headline: ISM manufacturing prices paid hit new cycle high in March; inflation pressures building
items.summary: Manufacturing input prices reached a new cycle peak in March per ISM data — signaling renewed inflationary pressure even as wage growth has moderated. Combined with Iran-driven oil spikes, the Fed faces a complicated picture ahead of its April meeting.

items.headline: Q1 2026 S&P 500 earnings growth projected at 13% — FactSet update
items.summary: FactSet's latest Q1 2026 projections peg S&P 500 earnings growth at 13%, led by strong tech and energy. Cyclicals — industrials, communication services, tech — led this week's gains. The earnings season gets fully underway in the coming weeks.

items.headline: Fed rate cut at April meeting appears unlikely despite cooling wages
items.summary: Despite the strong jobs report and moderating wages, the combination of above-target inflation signals, oil price spike driven by Iran, and healthy labor market makes a Fed rate cut at the April meeting highly unlikely.

items.headline: Market breadth remains historically weak: only 28% of S&P 500 stocks above 50-day moving average
items.summary: Despite strong index gains this week, market breadth is still historically narrow. Only 28% of S&P 500 stocks are above their 50-day moving averages — up from below 20% last week, but far below the 70%+ seen earlier this year.

items.headline: Mortgage rates tick up in March; spring homebuying season starts from disadvantaged position
items.summary: Mortgage rates rose through March on a combination of Iran war uncertainty and uneven economic data. With a Fed rate cut in April looking unlikely, spring homebuyers face a challenging rate environment heading into the peak buying season.

items.headline: Iran war Day 33: US F-15E Strike Eagle shot down; 1 crew rescued, 1 possibly captured by IRGC
items.summary: A US F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down over Iran on Friday morning — the first confirmed aircraft loss despite US claims of full air dominance. One crew member was rescued by US forces; a second remains missing and may have been captured by the IRGC. Iran claims two US planes were downed. 13 Americans have been killed since the war began.

items.headline: Trump: 'The assault on Iran hasn't even started' — threatens bridges, power plants
items.summary: President Trump escalated his rhetoric, threatening to strike Iranian bridges and power plants while insisting Iran can't do 'a thing' to stop potential strikes on oil infrastructure. His Wednesday prime-time address updated the nation on war aims; tensions show no sign of deescalating.

items.headline: US-Israel intensify strikes in Iran; targets include Tehran medical center and key bridges
items.summary: Day 33 of the war saw US-Israel strike a century-old Tehran medical research center, multiple bridges, and additional infrastructure. The campaign is expanding in scope and civilian proximity, intensifying international criticism.

items.headline: Strait of Hormuz closure fears deepen; Bahrain UN resolution called 'flawed and dangerous'
items.summary: The continued Strait of Hormuz closure is driving global oil spikes. The International Crisis Group criticized a Bahrain-proposed UN resolution, warning it 'treats a political crisis as if it can be solved at gunpoint' and risks further escalation.

items.headline: Eastern Europe tensions remain elevated as Iran war consumes global attention
items.summary: Analysts warn that the Iran war's dominance of geopolitical bandwidth risks reducing Western focus on the Eastern European theater, potentially emboldening further escalation there.

items.headline: Trump budget: $1.5T defense (+42%), TSA privatization begins, deep cuts to social programs
items.summary: The White House FY budget blueprint — a largely symbolic document — calls for $1.5T in defense (42% above current), funding munitions, naval expansion, and the 'Golden Dome' missile shield. Democrats are furious; some GOP skeptics exist. TSA screener privatization begins at small airports. Housing, healthcare, and social services face deep cuts.

items.headline: US F-15E downed over Iran: political and military stakes surge at home
items.summary: The first confirmed shoot-down of a US aircraft — despite months of claims of air dominance — is a political and military turning point. Trump was immediately briefed; rescue operations are ongoing. The incident undermines the administration's narrative on the war's progress.

items.headline: ICE inspectors find 49 violations at Camp East Montana — including self-harm prevention failures
items.summary: February inspections at Camp East Montana detention facility uncovered 49 violations to federal detention standards, including staff failures to 'accurately document required checks to prevent significant self-harm and suicide.'

items.headline: Vermont becomes first state with AI election law: synthetic media in campaigns now regulated
items.summary: Vermont's S.23 — restricting AI-generated synthetic media in election campaign materials — was signed into law after passing both chambers. It's among the first US state laws directly regulating AI in political advertising.

items.headline: NYT deep dive: AI is reshaping Silicon Valley before it reshapes the rest of the world
items.summary: A major New York Times feature explores how AI is already fundamentally changing tech work in Silicon Valley — from coding to legal work to radiology — even as the broader economic transformation has arrived more slowly than tech's big thinkers predicted.

items.headline: Arsenal injury crisis before FA Cup QF: Saka, Rice, Odegaard, Saliba all doubts for Southampton (Sat)
items.summary: Arsenal face Southampton in the FA Cup quarterfinal Saturday with a serious injury cloud: Saka (fatigue — precautionary), Rice (discomfort — doubtful), Odegaard and Saliba also uncertain. A total of 10 Arsenal players withdrew from the international break. Arteta is keeping cards close to chest on availability.
items.source: The Standard / Sports Mole / Football London

items.headline: Arsenal lead Premier League title race; statistical analysis shows even stronger position without set-piece goals
items.summary: Arsenal sit atop the Premier League table. New analysis reveals their open-play dominance is even stronger than the table suggests — remove set-piece goals and their lead over the field extends further, highlighting the quality of Arteta's system.

items.headline: Bucks officially eliminated from 2026 playoffs — first miss since 2015-16; lose to Celtics 147-129
items.summary: Milwaukee was blown out by Boston at Fiserv Forum, with Jaylen Brown dropping 43 points (58.6% shooting) in 37 minutes. The Bucks (30-46) are now officially out — their first playoff miss in a decade. The loss cements a deeply disappointing season.

items.headline: Giannis's future with Bucks in serious doubt — blockbuster offseason decisions loom
items.summary: With Milwaukee officially eliminated, questions about Giannis Antetokounmpo's long-term commitment are at a fever pitch. The Athletic reports real uncertainty over whether the Greek Freak will be in Milwaukee for training camp. The front office faces another make-or-break summer.

items.headline: Packers enter 2026 NFL Draft with 7 picks but no first-rounder; focus on pick 52 and new DC Jonathan Gannon
items.summary: Green Bay picks 52nd overall — no first-round pick this year or in 2027. GM Gutekunst has been explaining roster decisions; new defensive coordinator Jonathan Gannon is expected to transform the defense. The Packers are openly described as 'a work in progress' heading into the season.

items.headline: Aaron Rodgers expected to announce 2026 playing decision before NFL Draft
items.summary: Pittsburgh Steelers president Art Rooney II says he expects Aaron Rodgers to decide on continuing his career before the 2026 NFL Draft — a decision with ripple effects across multiple teams' quarterback and draft strategies.

items.headline: Luka Dončić suffers left hamstring injury in Lakers' 139-97 blowout loss to Thunder
items.summary: Lakers star Luka Dončić left Thursday night's game with a left hamstring injury after LA was crushed 139-97 by Oklahoma City. With the playoffs approaching, his availability timeline is uncertain — a significant concern for LA's postseason chances.

items.headline: NBA playoff picture: Pistons lead East; Celtics and Knicks in; regular season ends April 12
items.summary: Detroit Pistons hold the East's top seed. Boston Celtics (51-25) and New York Knicks have clinched playoff berths. The race for seeding and play-in spots intensifies over the final week. Kevin Garnett's jersey retirement is set for next season at Target Center.

items.headline: Liverpool reportedly putting 5 players up for sale as Arne Slot begins major squad overhaul
items.summary: Liverpool is reportedly making sweeping changes with five players on the transfer block as manager Arne Slot fully begins imposing his vision on the squad ahead of next season.

items.headline: NFL free agency grades: Kyler Murray to Vikings draws praise; Falcons unveil red-primary new uniforms
items.summary: NFL executives' roundtable gives positive marks to the Vikings-Kyler Murray signing ('Minnesota improved… how could you not?'). The Falcons unveiled new uniforms returning to a red primary color base, and the 2026 draft QB1 race is reportedly Fernando Mendoza (Indiana) over Ty Simpson (Alabama).

youtube.title: AI News: Anthropic Leak is Bigger Than You Think
youtube.creator: Matt Wolfe
youtube.summary: Matt Wolfe's weekly AI news roundup, centered on the Anthropic code leak and why its implications run deeper than initially reported. Also covers the week's major model releases, developer tool updates, and the AI landscape heading into April.

youtube.title: AI News: Anthropic Went Crazy This Week!
youtube.creator: Matt Wolfe
youtube.summary: Matt Wolfe's prior week AI roundup (March 28 week) covering Anthropic's major announcements, new model capabilities, tool releases, and developer ecosystem updates — good context for the week's leaked code revelations.
